agent_raw:
The board should not use the per-frame mAP improvement to support a nighttime braking safety statement. The benchmark has three gaps.

The headline evidence is detector mAP, but the claim is about nighttime emergency braking safety. These require different evidence. A per-frame box overlap metric does not measure whether the detector produces temporally stable, timely detections at the distances the braking planner requires.

The planner's trigger mechanism is specifically stated: a pedestrian must be detected at minimum stopping distance and remain present across a 400-millisecond temporal window. COCO-style per-frame mAP does not assess temporal persistence. A detector that improves box overlap on static frames may still produce unstable detections that fail the 400ms stability requirement.

The inference time rose from 24ms to 38ms on the production SoC. This is a 58% increase in per-frame latency. At real-time video rates, this either forces a lower frame rate or increases pipeline latency. Neither is reflected in the mAP headline. If the latency increase reduces effective stopping distance by delivering detections later, the safety claim may be weakened even as mAP improves.

Planner-in-the-loop testing is required to determine whether the mAP improvement translates to better braking outcomes.
all_issues_raised: Aggregate mAP does not measure nighttime braking safety — metric-to-claim gap
Per-frame COCO-style mAP does not assess 400ms temporal persistence requirement
38ms inference latency on production SoC not reflected in headline metric

empirical_test.condition: Planner-in-the-loop closed-track testing measuring nighttime braking success rate and stopping margin
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Planner-in-the-loop testing shows no improvement or worse nighttime braking outcomes due to temporal persistence and latency effects
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Planner-in-the-loop testing shows statistically significant improvement in nighttime braking success and stopping margin despite slower runtime
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Detector helps some nighttime scenarios but trades off against others, leaving overall safety impact unclear
